name: Salto del Nervion — circular from Untza
name_original: Salto del Nervión — circular desde Untza
region_intro: Hiking around Urkabustaiz offers varied terrain, situated between the Gorbeia Massif and the Orduña Crags, within the Gorbeia Natural Park and near the Sierra Sálvada mountain range. The region features extensive beech and oak forests, river valleys, and notable waterfalls such as Salto del Nervión and Goiuri-Ondona. Its landscape is characterized by diverse topography, from lush valleys to mountain peaks and karst formations. Hikers can explore a network of trails across these natural features. Best hiking trails around Urkabustaiz The most popular hiking route is Salto del Nervion — circular from Untza, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the impressive Salto del Nervión waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Nervión River – Monte Santiago Wolf Trap loop from Untza-Apregindana, a moderate 7.0 miles (11.2 km) path. This trail explores the Nervión River area and forested sections. Local hikers also love the Church of Izarra – Ostuño loop from Izarra, a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail leading through local villages and gentle hills, often completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes. Hiking around Urkabustaiz is defined by the Gorbeia Natural Park, the Sierra Sálvada mountain range, and spectacular waterfalls. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails. The routes in Urkabustaiz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Urkabustaiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Urkabustaiz?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails in Urkabustaiz, catering to various difficulty levels. You'll find a diverse network of paths, from easy strolls to more challenging treks, exploring the region's natural beauty.

Q: Which waterfalls can I see while hiking in Urkabustaiz?
A: Urkabustaiz is renowned for its spectacular waterfalls. The most famous is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3374993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Nervión&lt;/a&gt;, the highest waterfall on the Iberian Peninsula. You can also discover the impressive Goiuri-Ondona Waterfall, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4160547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corraladas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the Ebana Waterfall, considered the source of the Nervión River.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Urkabustaiz?
A: Yes, Urkabustaiz offers several excellent circular hiking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036035436/salto-del-nervion-circular-from-untza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Nervion — circular from Untza&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 12.4 km trail. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6973744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Izarra – Ostuño loop from Izarra&lt;/a&gt;, an easier 5.2 km route perfect for a casual walk.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in Urkabustaiz?
A: The terrain in Urkabustaiz is varied, ranging from lush river valleys to imposing mountain peaks. You'll hike through extensive beech and oak forests, encounter karst formations, and traverse paths near the Gorbeia Natural Park and the Sierra Sálvada mountain range. Expect a mix of forest paths, open trails, and some elevation changes, especially on routes leading to viewpoints or waterfalls.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Urkabustaiz?
A: Absolutely. Urkabustaiz provides several easy hiking options suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6973744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Izarra – Ostuño loop from Izarra&lt;/a&gt; is a gentle 5.2 km trail that passes through local villages and mild hills, ideal for a leisurely family outing.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints to look out for?
A: Beyond the waterfalls, hikers can enjoy several significant landmarks and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Pico del Fraile&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning vistas, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3239421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ganalto Summit Cross&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Oro&lt;/a&gt;, a cultural highlight, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2652013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Goros Caves&lt;/a&gt;, showcasing the area's karst geology.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Urkabustaiz?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's diverse natural landscapes, the spectacular waterfalls, and the well-maintained trails that offer options for all skill levels. Many appreciate the tranquility of the forests and the breathtaking views from various viewpoints.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Urkabustaiz?
A: Urkabustaiz offers great hiking opportunities throughout the year, but the best time often depends on your preferences. Spring (after heavy rainfall) and autumn are ideal for experiencing the waterfalls at their fullest and enjoying vibrant foliage. Summer provides pleasant temperatures for forest walks, while winter can offer unique, serene landscapes, especially after snowfall, though some trails might be more challenging.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly trails in Urkabustaiz?
A: Many trails in Urkabustaiz are suitable for hiking with dogs, allowing you to explore the natural beauty together. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Gorbeia Natural Park, and to respect local wildlife and other hikers. Always carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging hikes available?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Urkabustaiz offers more demanding routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38180300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corraladas Waterfall – Altube River Waterfall loop from Casa Arriba&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 22.4 km trail with significant elevation gain, exploring multiple waterfalls and the rugged landscape.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking?
A: Urkabustaiz, particularly within or near the Gorbeia Natural Park and Sierra Sálvada, is rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, including a significant colony of vultures near the Nervión viewpoint. The forests are home to deer, wild boar, and smaller mammals. Keep an eye out for diverse flora, especially in the extensive beech and oak groves.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: Parking is generally available at or near the main trailheads and popular starting points for hikes in Urkabustaiz, such as Untza or Izarra. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route descriptions on komoot, helping you plan your starting point effectively.
